mission.title: Install and Configure Falcosidekick (Falco) on Kubernetes
mission.description: Falcosidekick is a daemon that connects Falco to your ecosystem, forwarding Falco events to various outputs. Installing Falcosidekick allows you to enhance your security monitoring by integrating Falco alerts with your existing tools and workflows.

steps.title: Add the Falcosidekick Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the official Falcosidekick Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration with the following command:
```bash
helm repo add falcosidekick https://falcosecurity.github.io/charts
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install Falcosidekick using Helm
steps.description:
Install Falcosidekick in your Kubernetes cluster using the following command. This command creates a namespace called 'falcosidekick' if it doesn't exist:
```bash
helm install falcosidekick falcosidekick/falcosidekick --namespace falcosidekick --create-namespace --version 2.32.0
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check that the Falcosidekick pod is running by executing:
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace falcosidekick
```

steps.title: Configure resource limits
steps.description:
To ensure optimal performance, configure resource limits for the Falcosidekick deployment. Edit the deployment with:
```bash
kubectl edit deployment falcosidekick --namespace falcosidekick
```
Then, add the following resource limits under the containers section:
```yaml
resources:
  limits:
    memory: "256Mi"
    cpu: "500m"
  requests:
    memory: "128Mi"
    cpu: "250m"
```

steps.title: Set up port forwarding for access
steps.description:
To access the Falcosidekick service, set up port forwarding with the following command:
```bash
kubectl port-forward svc/falcosidekick --namespace falcosidekick 2801:2801
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of Falcosidekick will show the pod running in the 'falcosidekick' namespace, and you will be able to access the service via port forwarding. Additionally, resource limits will be configured to ensure efficient operation.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add falcosidekick https://falcosecurity.github.io/charts
helm repo update

resources:
  limits:
    memory: "256Mi"
    cpu: "500m"
  requests:
    memory: "128Mi"
    cpu: "250m"

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with Helm and kubectl installed.
